query: I want a rule that denies any `git push --force` (or `-f`) targeting the main or master branch, across all my projects. Walk me through writing it as a rule file rather than burying it in settings.json.
expected_behavior: Recommends a .claude/rules/<rule-slug>.md file (user scope for cross-project coverage) and shows the file structure — matcher pattern, decision (deny), severity, and a rationale section explaining WHY the rule exists

Writes a matcher that catches both --force and -f variants on main and master (e.g., a Bash regex or a structured matcher covering `git push (--force|-f).*\b(main|master)\b`), not a brittle exact-string match
Cross-references the relationship between declarative rules and PreToolUse hooks — rules describe WHAT is allowed/denied; hooks (or the built-in rule evaluator) enforce them at runtime
